extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco obliterated the field by 48 seconds over Roglič in a 19-mile downhill TT, averaging 54km/h to extend his GC lead.
tactical_patterns: BikeExchange riders (Hepburn, Durbridge) dominated early splits before GC men started, suggesting specialized TT squads calibrate pacing better downhill.
Evenepoel's 48-second margin over Roglič on flat terrain reveals VO2max advantage that negates climbing specialists' weight benefits in pure power disciplines.
Young GC contenders (Evenepoel 22, Rodriguez mentioned) showing TT prowess suggests new generation prioritizes multi-discipline training over pure climbing specialization.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: 48s gap to Roglič on flat TT signals he can extend GC leads on any power-based terrain, not just mountains where weight matters less
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: Losing 48s to Evenepoel while posting second-fastest time suggests his TT superiority window has closed against younger power riders
rider_signals.RODRIGUEZ Carlos: neutral: Mentioned as surprising youngster in top-4 GC, indicating unknown quantity who may lack TT discipline to defend mountain gains
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: Dropped to 50s back after TT, confirming pure climbers without TT capacity cannot defend mountain gains in stage races
